rationale: The cited paper’s core constructs (Bayesian learning from detailing versus direct goodwill effects in physician prescription choice) closely match the citing paper’s focal constructs (Bayesian learning, informative/persuasive detailing effects, and adoption timing). The citing note says it extends prior pharmaceutical diffusion work by combining learning channels with forward-looking behavior, making this 2005 structural detailing/learning study a plausible substantive source rather than a generic mention. The reference is also a direct DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.2005.42.3.278), so it should be kept for full relation judgment.
